title: teucrium scorodonia subsp. siculum
intro: Teucrium scorodonia subsp. siculum, also known as Sicilian wood sage, is a subspecies of the Teucrium scorodonia plant, which belongs to the lamiaceae family and is a type of perennial herb. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Sicilian wood sage include its ability to reduce stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Teucrium scorodonia subsp. siculum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
